question: For completed FileWrite operations grouped into simulation time buckets of 500 time units, what is the write count, total bytes written, average write duration, average site storage utilization, and average grid storage utilization per bucket, revealing how disk write pressure and storage capacity consumption evolve across the simulation timeline?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 500 AS INTEGER) * 500 AS time_bucket_start, COUNT(*) AS write_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY time_bucket_start ORDER BY time_bucket_start

question: For completed job executions grouped into core count buckets (exactly 1 core, 2 to 4 cores, 5 to 8 cores, and more than 8 cores), what is the job count, average flops, average duration, average cost, average site CPU utilization, and average grid CPU utilization, revealing whether higher-core allocations improve job throughput and how they influence CPU pressure across the grid?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN '1_core' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 2 AND 4 THEN '2_to_4_cores' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 5 AND 8 THEN '5_to_8_cores' ELSE 'more_than_8_cores' END AS core_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY core_bucket ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER))

question: For each combination of site and disk in completed FileRead operations, what is the average declared disk_read_bw, average effective read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average efficiency ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth), minimum and maximum efficiency ratio, and total read count — revealing which site-disk combinations most consistently fail to reach their rated read bandwidth?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS read_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_bw, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_efficiency_ratio, MIN((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS min_efficiency_ratio, MAX((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_efficiency_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY site, disk ORDER BY avg_efficiency_ratio ASC

question: For each execution site in completed job executions, what is the average cost per flop (cost divided by flops), total flops processed, total cost, average cost per job, and job count — revealing which sites deliver the most economical computation per unit of work and which are disproportionately expensive relative to the compute they provide?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS total_flops, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost_per_job, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost_per_flop, MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS min_cost_per_flop, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS max_cost_per_flop FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_cost_per_flop ASC

question: For completed job executions grouped into four file_transfer_queue_time buckets (exactly zero, under 10, 10 to 100, and over 100 time units), what is the job count, average total duration, average file_transfer_queue_time, average resource_waiting_queue_time, average cost, and average retries — revealing how data-staging delays associate with broader job characteristics and whether they co-occur with elevated resource contention?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) = 0 THEN 'zero' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) < 10 THEN 'under_10' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) < 100 THEN '10_to_100' ELSE 'over_100' END AS transfer_queue_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_waiting_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY transfer_queue_bucket ORDER BY AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL))

question: For completed job executions grouped into four flop-complexity buckets (under 1000000000, 1000000000 to 10000000000, 10000000000 to 100000000000, and over 100000000000 flops), what is the job count, average retries, fraction of jobs requiring at least one retry, average duration, average cost, and average speed — revealing whether more compute-intensive jobs are more failure-prone and whether retries systematically inflate their cost?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'under_1G' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) < 10000000000 THEN '1G_to_10G' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) < 100000000000 THEN '10G_to_100G' ELSE 'over_100G' END AS flops_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS retry_fraction, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) AS avg_speed FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY flops_bucket ORDER BY AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL))

question: For the top 10 output files by write frequency across all completed FileWrite operations, what is the total write count, total bytes written, average write duration, average effective write throughput in bytes per second, number of distinct sites where writes occurred, and number of distinct disk devices used — revealing which output files form the heaviest and most widely distributed write workload across the storage infrastructure?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, COUNT(*) AS write_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_write_bw, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk')) AS distinct_disks FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY file_name ORDER BY write_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: For each site, what is the average total end-to-end job lifetime (from JobAllocation Started simulation time to JobExecution Finished simulation time), the average allocation phase duration, average total queue time, average pure execution duration, and each phase as a percentage of the total lifetime — revealing which sites have the most overhead-heavy job lifecycles relative to productive computation?
sql: SELECT json_extract(je_fin.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(je_fin.TIME - ja_start.TIME) AS avg_end_to_end_time, AVG(ja_fin.TIME - ja_start.TIME) AS avg_allocation_phase_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(je_fin.METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(je_fin.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, AVG(100.0 * (ja_fin.TIME - ja_start.TIME) / NULLIF(je_fin.TIME - ja_start.TIME, 0)) AS pct_time_in_allocation, AVG(100.0 * CAST(json_extract(je_fin.METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) / NULLIF(je_fin.TIME - ja_start.TIME, 0)) AS pct_time_in_queue, AVG(100.0 * CAST(json_extract(je_fin.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) / NULLIF(je_fin.TIME - ja_start.TIME, 0)) AS pct_time_in_execution FROM EVENTS ja_start JOIN EVENTS ja_fin ON ja_start.JOB_ID = ja_fin.JOB_ID AND ja_start.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND ja_start.STATE = 'Started' AND ja_fin.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND ja_fin.STATE = 'Finished' JOIN EVENTS je_fin ON ja_start.JOB_ID = je_fin.JOB_ID AND je_fin.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND je_fin.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_end_to_end_time DESC

question: For each site, what is the average site_cpu_util and grid_cpu_util recorded at JobExecution Started events versus at JobExecution Finished events for the same jobs, and the average change in each metric between those two states — revealing whether CPU pressure at the site and grid level systematically rises or falls between a job's launch and its completion?
sql: SELECT json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_start, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_finish, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_change, AVG(CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_start, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_finish, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_change FROM EVENTS s JOIN EVENTS f ON s.JOB_ID = f.JOB_ID AND s.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND s.STATE = 'Started' AND f.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND f.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_cpu_change DESC

question: For completed file transfers grouped by destination site, what is the total transfer count, total bytes received, average and maximum site_storage_util at transfer completion, average grid_storage_util at transfer completion, and average transfer duration — revealing which destination sites face the greatest storage saturation pressure from inbound data movement across the grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_received, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY destination_site ORDER BY avg_site_storage_util DESC

question: For completed FileRead operations grouped by site and 1000-time-unit simulation clock buckets, what is the read count, total bytes read, average read duration, average effective read throughput in bytes per second, and number of distinct disk devices active — revealing when and where peak disk read demand occurs across sites throughout the simulation timeline?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CAST(TIME / 1000 AS INTEGER) * 1000 AS time_bucket_start, COUNT(*) AS read_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_throughput_bytes_per_sec, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk')) AS distinct_disks_active FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, time_bucket_start ORDER BY site, time_bucket_start

question: For completed job executions grouped by site and retry count bucket (0 retries, 1 retry, 2 to 3 retries, 4 to 5 retries, and 6 or more retries), what is the job count, average cost, average duration, average total queue time, and total cost — revealing the retry severity distribution at each site and whether higher retry counts systematically associate with greater cost and queuing overhead?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN '0' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN '1' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 2 AND 3 THEN '2-3' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 4 AND 5 THEN '4-5' ELSE '6+' END AS retry_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, retry_bucket ORDER BY site, retry_bucket

question: For each site, what is the total bytes read versus total bytes written across all completed FileRead and FileWrite operations, the read-to-write byte ratio, the operation counts for each type, and the average duration for reads versus writes — revealing whether sites act primarily as data consumers or data producers?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN 1 END) AS read_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN 1 END) AS write_count, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_written, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END), 0) AS read_write_byte_ratio, AVG(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) END) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) END) AS avg_write_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE STATE = 'Finished' AND EVENT IN ('FileRead', 'FileWrite') GROUP BY site ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC;

question: For each site, what is the average, minimum, and maximum duration of the job allocation phase (measured as the elapsed simulation time between JobAllocation Started and JobAllocation Finished for the same job), along with the allocation count and average site_cpu_util and grid_cpu_util recorded at allocation finish — revealing which sites suffer the longest and most variable resource-matching delays before jobs can even begin?
sql: SELECT json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS avg_allocation_phase_duration, MIN(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS min_allocation_phase_duration, MAX(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS max_allocation_phase_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_finish, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_finish FROM EVENTS f JOIN EVENTS s ON f.JOB_ID = s.JOB_ID AND s.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND s.STATE = 'Started' WHERE f.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND f.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_allocation_phase_duration DESC;

question: For each source site across completed FileTransfer events, what is the average network latency, average transfer duration, average file size in bytes, total transfer count, and average ratio of latency to total duration — revealing which source sites are dominated by network propagation delay rather than bandwidth-limited transmission time?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS avg_latency, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_latency_fraction_of_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site ORDER BY avg_latency_fraction_of_duration DESC;

question: For each host and execution site in completed JobExecution events, what is the average, minimum, and maximum declared compute speed, the range between max and min speed, the average flops processed per job, and the total job count — revealing which hosts exhibit the greatest speed variability across jobs, potentially indicating heterogeneous hardware assignments or dynamic frequency scaling?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) AS avg_speed, MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) AS min_speed, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) AS max_speed, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) - MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) AS speed_range, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops_per_job FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host, site ORDER BY speed_range DESC;

question: For each site and file-size bucket (under 1000000 bytes, 1000000 to 100000000 bytes, 100000000 to 1000000000 bytes, and over 1000000000 bytes) in completed FileWrite operations, what is the write count, average file size in bytes, average write duration, and average effective write throughput in bytes per second — revealing how write workload size distribution differs across sites and whether larger writes achieve proportionally higher throughput?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000 THEN 'A_under_1000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 100000000 THEN 'B_1000000_to_100000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'C_100000000_to_1000000000' ELSE 'D_over_1000000000' END AS size_bucket, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_write_throughput_bps FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, size_bucket ORDER BY site, size_bucket;

question: For completed FileRead operations grouped into four file-size buckets (under 1000000 bytes, 1000000 to 100000000 bytes, 100000000 to 1000000000 bytes, and over 1000000000 bytes), what is the read count, average read duration, average effective read throughput in bytes per second, number of distinct disk devices used, and number of distinct sites involved — revealing how file size influences read performance and which storage tiers serve each read size class?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000 THEN 'under_1000000_bytes' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) < 100000000 THEN '1000000_to_100000000_bytes' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN '100000000_to_1000000000_bytes' ELSE 'over_1000000000_bytes' END AS size_bucket, COUNT(*) AS read_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL),0)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bytes_per_sec, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk')) AS distinct_disk_devices, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileRead' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY size_bucket ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL))

question: For each source-to-destination site pair in completed FileTransfer events, what is the transfer count, average declared network bandwidth in bytes per second, average effective throughput achieved in bytes per second, average bandwidth efficiency ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth), and total bytes transferred — revealing which inter-site network paths most severely underperform their provisioned capacity?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA,'$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL),0)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bytes_per_sec, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL),0)) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.bandwidth') AS REAL),0)) AS avg_bandwidth_efficiency_ratio, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileTransfer' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY avg_bandwidth_efficiency_ratio ASC

question: For completed job executions grouped into four host compute speed tiers (under 1000000000, 1000000000 to 10000000000, 10000000000 to 100000000000, and over 100000000000 flops per second), what is the job count, average flops, average duration, average cost, average cores, average site CPU utilization, and average grid CPU utilization — revealing how host compute speed relates to job completion time, cost efficiency, and overall system load?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.speed') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'under_1000000000_flops_per_sec' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.speed') AS REAL) < 10000000000 THEN '1000000000_to_10000000000_flops_per_sec' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.speed') AS REAL) < 100000000000 THEN '10000000000_to_100000000000_flops_per_sec' ELSE 'over_100000000000_flops_per_sec' END AS speed_tier, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_cores, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='JobExecution' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY speed_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.speed') AS REAL))

question: For the top 10 files by total bytes transferred across all completed FileTransfer events, what is the transfer count, total bytes transferred, average file size in bytes, average transfer duration, average effective throughput in bytes per second, and the number of distinct source-to-destination route pairs involved — revealing which files dominate inter-site network traffic and how widely their transfers are distributed across the grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.file') AS file_name, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL),0)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bytes_per_sec, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.source_site') || '->' || json_extract(METADATA,'$.destination_site')) AS distinct_route_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileTransfer' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY file_name ORDER BY total_bytes_transferred DESC LIMIT 10

question: For completed job executions grouped by site and by whether resource_waiting_queue_time is exactly zero versus greater than zero, what is the job count, average duration, average cost, average retries, average site CPU utilization, and average grid CPU utilization — revealing how CPU resource contention at each site influences job execution outcomes and failure rates?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) = 0 THEN 'no_resource_wait' ELSE 'has_resource_wait' END AS wait_category, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='JobExecution' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY site, wait_category ORDER BY site, wait_category

question: For each destination site in completed FileTransfer events grouped by link_load tier at finish (0 to 0.25, 0.25 to 0.5, 0.5 to 0.75, and 0.75 to 1.0), what is the transfer count, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average declared bandwidth, bandwidth efficiency ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth), and average transfer duration — revealing how inbound network congestion at each destination site impacts actual data delivery rates across the grid?
sql:
SELECT
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site,
    CASE
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.25 THEN '0.00_to_0.25'
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.50 THEN '0.25_to_0.50'
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.75 THEN '0.50_to_0.75'
        ELSE '0.75_to_1.00'
    END AS link_load_tier,
    COUNT(*) AS transfer_count,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bps,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth_bps,
    AVG(
        CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) /
        NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL), 0)
    ) AS avg_bandwidth_efficiency,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY destination_site, link_load_tier
ORDER BY destination_site, link_load_tier;

question: For each site and disk device combining all completed FileRead and FileWrite operations, what is the read count, write count, total bytes read, total bytes written, average effective read throughput in bytes per second, average effective write throughput in bytes per second, and the read-to-write operation ratio — revealing which disk devices at each site are read-dominated versus write-dominated and how their throughput compares across both operation types?
sql:
SELECT
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS read_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS write_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_written,
    AVG(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead'
        THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)
        END) AS avg_read_throughput_bps,
    AVG(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite'
        THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)
        END) AS avg_write_throughput_bps,
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) /
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS read_to_write_op_ratio
FROM EVENTS
WHERE STATE = 'Finished' AND EVENT IN ('FileRead', 'FileWrite')
GROUP BY site, disk
ORDER BY site, disk;

question: For completed job executions grouped by site and by total_queue_time severity bucket (exactly zero, 0 to 50, 50 to 200, and over 200 time units), what is the job count, average duration, average cost, average retries, and fraction of that site's total jobs falling in each bucket — revealing how queueing delay severity at each site correlates with execution cost and failure rates?
sql:
SELECT
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
    CASE
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) = 0 THEN 'zero'
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) <= 50 THEN '0_to_50'
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) <= 200 THEN '50_to_200'
        ELSE 'over_200'
    END AS total_queue_time_bucket,
    COUNT(*) AS job_count,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries,
    CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (PARTITION BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS fraction_of_site_jobs
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY site, total_queue_time_bucket
ORDER BY site, total_queue_time_bucket;

question: For each site and host in completed JobExecution events, what is the average, minimum, and maximum compute efficiency (flops divided by the product of duration and declared speed, representing the fraction of rated capacity actually delivered), the average core count, and the total job count — revealing which host-site combinations most consistently underperform their rated compute capacity and which sustain near-peak efficiency?
sql:
SELECT
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host,
    COUNT(*) AS job_count,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_cores,
    AVG(
        CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) /
        NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL), 0)
    ) AS avg_compute_efficiency,
    MIN(
        CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) /
        NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL), 0)
    ) AS min_compute_efficiency,
    MAX(
        CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) /
        NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL), 0)
    ) AS max_compute_efficiency
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY site, host
ORDER BY avg_compute_efficiency ASC;

question: For completed job executions grouped into five buckets based on the fraction of total duration spent queuing (total_queue_time divided by duration: under 10%, 10 to 25%, 25 to 50%, 50 to 75%, and over 75%), what is the job count, average net compute duration (duration minus total_queue_time), average cost, average retries, and number of distinct sites represented — revealing what share of the job fleet is dominated by queueing overhead versus productive computation and whether queue-heavy jobs incur disproportionate cost and failures?
sql:
SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) /
             NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0) < 0.10 THEN 'under_10pct'
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) /
             NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0) < 0.25 THEN '10_to_25pct'
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) /
             NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0) < 0.50 THEN '25_to_50pct'
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) /
             NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0) < 0.75 THEN '50_to_75pct'
        ELSE 'over_75pct'
    END AS queue_fraction_bucket,
    COUNT(*) AS job_count,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_net_compute_duration,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries,
    COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_site_count
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY queue_fraction_bucket
ORDER BY queue_fraction_bucket;

question: For completed job executions where total_io_read_time is greater than zero, grouped by site and by the fraction of total duration spent on IO reads (under 25%, 25% to 50%, 50% to 75%, and over 75%), what is the job count, average duration, average cost, average retries, and average file_transfer_queue_time — revealing which sites suffer the most IO-dominated job patterns and whether IO intensity correlates with cost and failure rates?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) < 0.25 THEN 'under_25pct' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) < 0.50 THEN '25_to_50pct' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) < 0.75 THEN '50_to_75pct' ELSE 'over_75pct' END AS io_fraction_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_queue_time FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY site, io_fraction_bucket ORDER BY site, io_fraction_bucket

question: For each site across all completed JobAllocation events, what is the total allocation count, average site_storage_util, average grid_storage_util, average site_cpu_util, average grid_cpu_util, and maximum site_storage_util — revealing how heavily loaded each site's storage and CPU resources are at the moment jobs finish being allocated and which sites face the most capacity pressure during job placement?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_storage_util DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by network latency tier (under 0.001, 0.001 to 0.01, 0.01 to 0.1, and over 0.1 time units), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average transfer duration, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average declared bandwidth, and number of distinct source-to-destination site pairs — revealing how network propagation delay tiers correspond to actual delivery performance and which latency classes serve the widest range of grid routes?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.001 THEN 'under_0.001' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.01 THEN '0.001_to_0.01' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.1 THEN '0.01_to_0.1' ELSE 'over_0.1' END AS latency_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth_bps, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') || '->' || json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site')) AS distinct_site_pairs FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY latency_tier ORDER BY latency_tier

question: For each host and execution site in completed JobExecution events, what is the total job count, average flops per job, sum of all flops processed, average duration, average cores, and average cost — revealing which individual host machines carry the heaviest computational workload across the simulation and whether job characteristics vary significantly across hosts sharing the same site?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_jobs, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops_per_job, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS total_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_cores, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host, site ORDER BY total_flops DESC

question: For completed FileWrite operations grouped by site and disk device, what is the write count, average declared disk_write_bw, average effective write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average efficiency ratio (effective throughput divided by declared disk_write_bw), minimum efficiency ratio, and maximum efficiency ratio — revealing which site-disk combinations most severely and most consistently underperform their rated write bandwidth across the storage infrastructure?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_write_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_write_throughput_bps, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_efficiency_ratio, MIN((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL), 0)) AS min_efficiency_ratio, MAX((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL), 0)) AS max_efficiency_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, disk ORDER BY avg_efficiency_ratio ASC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by core count, what is the job count, average flops per job, average duration, average cost, average cost-per-core (cost divided by cores), and average grid CPU utilization — revealing whether higher-core jobs achieve better economics and how parallelism level correlates with system-wide CPU load?
sql: SELECT CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) AS cores, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost_per_core, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY cores ORDER BY cores

question: For FileTransfer Started events grouped by source_site and destination_site, what is the transfer count, average link_load, maximum link_load, average declared bandwidth, and average file size in bytes at the moment each transfer is initiated — revealing which inter-site routes already face the most severe congestion before new transfers even begin?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load_at_start, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS max_link_load_at_start, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY avg_link_load_at_start DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped into simulation-time completion buckets of 5000 time units, what is the job count, average duration, average cost, average retries, and average grid_cpu_util — revealing how job difficulty, expense, failure rate, and grid load evolve across the simulation timeline?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 5000 AS INTEGER) * 5000 AS time_bucket_start, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY time_bucket_start ORDER BY time_bucket_start

question: For completed FileRead operations grouped by host and site, what is the read count, total bytes read, average declared disk_read_bw, average effective read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average efficiency ratio (effective throughput divided by declared disk_read_bw), and count of distinct disk devices used — revealing which individual compute hosts generate the heaviest disk read demand and whether any hosts show systematic read performance degradation across their attached storage?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS read_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_read_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_efficiency_ratio, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk')) AS distinct_disks FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host, site ORDER BY read_count DESC

question: For each site, what is the average, minimum, and maximum simulation-time gap between JobAllocation Finished and JobExecution Started for the same job, along with the total job count and the fraction of jobs where that gap exceeds 10 time units — revealing which sites impose the greatest scheduling lag between resource assignment and actual execution launch?
sql: SELECT json_extract(ja.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(je.TIME - ja.TIME) AS avg_gap, MIN(je.TIME - ja.TIME) AS min_gap, MAX(je.TIME - ja.TIME) AS max_gap, SUM(CASE WHEN (je.TIME - ja.TIME) > 10 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) AS fraction_gap_over_10 FROM EVENTS ja JOIN EVENTS je ON ja.JOB_ID = je.JOB_ID AND je.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND je.STATE = 'Started' WHERE ja.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND ja.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_gap DESC

question: For each site in completed JobExecution events, what is the job count, average cost, average flops per job, average cost per flop (cost divided by flops), total cost, and total flops — revealing which sites deliver the most and least cost-efficient compute and how site pricing models relate to the volume of work processed?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost_per_flop, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS total_flops FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_cost_per_flop ASC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped into simulation-time completion buckets of 2000 time units, what is the transfer count, total bytes transferred, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average link_load, average transfer duration, and number of distinct destination sites — revealing how inter-site data movement volume, network throughput, and congestion levels evolve across the simulation timeline?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 2000 AS INTEGER) * 2000 AS time_bucket_start, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site')) AS distinct_destination_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY time_bucket_start ORDER BY time_bucket_start

question: For the 20 individual jobs with the highest retry counts among completed JobExecution events, what is the site, host, retry count, duration, cost, total_queue_time, total_io_read_time, and file_transfer_queue_time — revealing whether the most failure-prone jobs share common site or host assignments and whether high retry rates coincide with elevated queue times or I/O overhead?
sql: SELECT JOB_ID, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) AS retries, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS duration, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) AS cost, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) AS total_queue_time, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) AS total_io_read_time, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) AS file_transfer_queue_time FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 ORDER BY retries DESC LIMIT 20

question: For completed FileRead operations grouped by declared disk_read_bw tier (under 100000000 bytes/s, 100000000 to 500000000 bytes/s, 500000000 to 1000000000 bytes/s, and over 1000000000 bytes/s), what is the read count, average file size in bytes, average declared disk_read_bw, average effective read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average read efficiency ratio (effective throughput divided by declared disk_read_bw), and number of distinct sites — revealing how well actual read performance scales with declared disk speed and whether faster storage tiers consistently achieve higher efficiency?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL) < 100000000 THEN 'under_100000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL) < 500000000 THEN '100000000_to_500000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN '500000000_to_1000000000' ELSE 'over_1000000000' END AS disk_read_bw_tier, COUNT(*) AS read_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_disk_read_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_read_throughput, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_efficiency_ratio, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL) > 0 AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY disk_read_bw_tier ORDER BY avg_declared_disk_read_bw

question: For jobs that have both completed FileTransfer and JobExecution events joined by JOB_ID, grouped by execution site, what is the job count, average total file transfer duration per job, average job execution duration, average ratio of total transfer duration to execution duration, and average total bytes transferred per job — revealing which sites' jobs spend the greatest fraction of execution time on inter-site data movement versus actual computation?
sql: SELECT je.site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(ft.total_transfer_duration) AS avg_total_file_transfer_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(je.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, AVG(ft.total_transfer_duration / CAST(json_extract(je.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_to_execution_ratio, AVG(ft.total_bytes) AS avg_total_bytes_transferred FROM (SELECT JOB_ID, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, METADATA FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished') AS je JOIN (SELECT JOB_ID, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS total_transfer_duration, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY JOB_ID) AS ft ON je.JOB_ID = ft.JOB_ID GROUP BY je.site ORDER BY avg_transfer_to_execution_ratio DESC

question: For completed FileWrite operations grouped by site and simulation-time completion bucket of 3000 time units, what is the write count, total bytes written, average effective write throughput in bytes per second, average grid_storage_util, and maximum site_storage_util — revealing how storage write pressure and throughput evolve over simulation time at each site and whether storage utilization peaks at predictable phases of the workload?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CAST(TIME / 3000 AS INTEGER) * 3000 AS time_bucket_start, COUNT(*) AS write_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_write_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, time_bucket_start ORDER BY site, time_bucket_start

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by retry count bucket (0 retries, 1 to 2 retries, 3 to 5 retries, and over 5 retries), what is the job count, average duration, average cost, average total_io_read_time, average file_transfer_queue_time, average resource_waiting_queue_time, and number of distinct sites — revealing which execution characteristics most strongly distinguish heavily-retried jobs from single-attempt completions and whether high failure rates correlate with I/O or queue delays?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN '0_retries' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 2 THEN '1_to_2_retries' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 3 AND 5 THEN '3_to_5_retries' ELSE 'over_5_retries' END AS retry_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_io_read_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_waiting_queue_time, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY retry_bucket ORDER BY retry_bucket

question: For each site comparing inbound and outbound traffic across all completed FileTransfer events, what is the outbound transfer count, total bytes sent, average outbound duration, inbound transfer count, total bytes received, average inbound duration, and the inbound-to-outbound byte ratio — revealing whether each site acts predominantly as a data exporter or importer and how transfer volumes are balanced across the grid?
sql: SELECT site, SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'source' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS outbound_count, SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'source' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_sent, AVG(CASE WHEN role = 'source' THEN duration ELSE NULL END) AS avg_outbound_duration, SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'destination' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS inbound_count, SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'destination' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_received, AVG(CASE WHEN role = 'destination' THEN duration ELSE NULL END) AS avg_inbound_duration, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'destination' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN role = 'source' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END), 0) AS inbound_to_outbound_byte_ratio FROM (SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS site, 'source' AS role, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS file_size, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' UNION ALL SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS site, 'destination' AS role, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS file_size, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished') sub GROUP BY site ORDER BY total_bytes_received DESC

question: For JobExecution Started events grouped by site and simulation-time start bucket of 5000 time units, what is the job start count, average site_cpu_util, average grid_cpu_util, average flops per job, and peak site_cpu_util — revealing how CPU utilization and incoming computational workload intensity at each site evolve across the simulation timeline and whether CPU pressure builds gradually or in bursts?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CAST(TIME / 5000 AS INTEGER) * 5000 AS time_bucket_start, COUNT(*) AS job_start_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops_per_job, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS peak_site_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY site, time_bucket_start ORDER BY site, time_bucket_start

question: For the top 20 files by combined access volume appearing in both completed FileRead and completed FileTransfer events, what is the local read count, remote transfer count, total bytes read locally, total bytes transferred remotely, average local read duration, average remote transfer duration, average local read throughput in bytes per second, average remote transfer throughput in bytes per second, and local-to-remote operation ratio — revealing which files are predominantly served from local disk versus inter-site movement and how local versus remote access performance compares for the same data objects?
sql: SELECT file_name, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS read_count, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileTransfer' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS transfer_count, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read_locally, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileTransfer' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_transferred_remotely, AVG(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN duration ELSE NULL END) AS avg_local_read_duration, AVG(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileTransfer' THEN duration ELSE NULL END) AS avg_remote_transfer_duration, AVG(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN file_size / NULLIF(duration, 0) ELSE NULL END) AS avg_local_read_throughput, AVG(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileTransfer' THEN file_size / NULLIF(duration, 0) ELSE NULL END) AS avg_remote_transfer_throughput, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileTransfer' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS local_to_remote_op_ratio FROM (SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, 'FileRead' AS event_type, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS file_size, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' UNION ALL SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, 'FileTransfer' AS event_type, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS file_size, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished') combined GROUP BY file_name HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 AND SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileTransfer' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 ORDER BY (SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) + SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileTransfer' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END)) DESC LIMIT 20

question: For completed FileWrite operations grouped by declared disk_write_bw tier (under 100000000 bytes/s, 100000000 to 500000000 bytes/s, 500000000 to 1000000000 bytes/s, and over 1000000000 bytes/s), what is the write count, average file size in bytes, average declared write bandwidth, average effective write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average efficiency ratio (effective throughput divided by declared disk_write_bw), and number of distinct sites — revealing how well actual write performance scales with rated disk speed and whether faster storage tiers consistently achieve higher write efficiency?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL) < 100000000 THEN 'under_100000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL) < 500000000 THEN '100000000_to_500000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN '500000000_to_1000000000' ELSE 'over_1000000000' END AS write_bw_tier, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_write_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_write_throughput, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_efficiency_ratio, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileWrite' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY write_bw_tier ORDER BY AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL))

question: For completed JobExecution events where total_queue_time is greater than zero, grouped by site and by the fraction of total_queue_time attributable to file_transfer_queue_time (under 25%, 25 to 50%, 50 to 75%, and over 75%), what is the job count, average duration, average cost, average retries, and average resource_waiting_queue_time — revealing which sites' job queuing is dominated by inter-site file transfer delays versus CPU resource contention and whether the dominant bottleneck type correlates with worse execution outcomes?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) = 0 THEN 'no_queue_time' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) < 0.25 THEN 'under_25pct_transfer' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) < 0.50 THEN '25_to_50pct_transfer' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) < 0.75 THEN '50_to_75pct_transfer' ELSE 'over_75pct_transfer' END AS ft_fraction_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_waiting_queue_time FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='JobExecution' AND STATE='Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY site, ft_fraction_bucket ORDER BY site, job_count DESC

question: For completed FileRead operations grouped into simulation-time completion buckets of 2000 time units, what is the read count, total bytes read, average effective read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average declared disk_read_bw, average read efficiency ratio (effective throughput divided by declared disk_read_bw), and number of distinct sites active — revealing how local disk read activity, throughput, and storage efficiency evolve across the simulation timeline and whether read pressure is concentrated in specific phases of the workload?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 2000 AS INTEGER) * 2000 AS time_bucket_start, COUNT(*) AS read_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_read_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_disk_read_bw, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_efficiency_ratio, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileRead' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY time_bucket_start ORDER BY time_bucket_start

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by destination site and file size tier (under 1000000 bytes, 1000000 to 1000000000 bytes, 1000000000 to 10000000000 bytes, and over 10000000000 bytes), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average transfer duration, average declared bandwidth, and number of distinct source sites — revealing what file size classes each destination site predominantly receives and whether large inbound transfers achieve proportionally better or worse throughput than small ones?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.destination_site') AS destination_site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000 THEN 'under_1000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN '1000000_to_1000000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) < 10000000000 THEN '1000000000_to_10000000000' ELSE 'over_10000000000' END AS file_size_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.source_site')) AS distinct_source_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileTransfer' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY destination_site, file_size_tier ORDER BY destination_site, avg_file_size_bytes

question: For each site and resulting allocation status, what is the count of completed JobAllocation lifecycles, average time elapsed from allocation Started to allocation Finished (using the TIME column of each event), minimum and maximum allocation elapsed time, and average site_cpu_util recorded at allocation finish — revealing which sites take the longest to complete job placement decisions, whether allocation latency varies by outcome status, and whether CPU load at the site correlates with slower or faster allocation resolution?
sql: SELECT json_extract(f.METADATA,'$.site') AS site, f.STATUS AS allocation_status, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS avg_allocation_elapsed, MIN(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS min_allocation_elapsed, MAX(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS max_allocation_elapsed, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_finish FROM EVENTS f JOIN EVENTS s ON f.JOB_ID = s.JOB_ID AND s.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND s.STATE = 'Started' WHERE f.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND f.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, allocation_status ORDER BY site, allocation_count DESC

question: For each source-to-destination site route in completed FileTransfer events, what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, total bytes transferred, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average link_load at completion — revealing which specific inter-site routes carry the heaviest traffic volume and whether high-traffic routes maintain acceptable throughput or suffer from sustained congestion?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY total_bytes_transferred DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events joined with their corresponding completed FileRead events by JOB_ID, grouped by execution site, what is the job count with associated reads, average number of file read operations per job, average total bytes read per job, average total_io_read_time, average job duration, and average fraction of duration attributable to IO reads — revealing which sites run the most read-intensive workloads and whether higher per-job read volume consistently drives up total execution time?
sql: SELECT json_extract(je.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(DISTINCT je.JOB_ID) AS job_count, AVG(rs.read_count) AS avg_reads_per_job, AVG(rs.total_bytes_read) AS avg_bytes_read_per_job, AVG(CAST(json_extract(je.METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_io_read_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(je.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(je.METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(je.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_io_read_fraction FROM EVENTS je JOIN (SELECT JOB_ID, COUNT(*) AS read_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY JOB_ID) rs ON je.JOB_ID = rs.JOB_ID WHERE je.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND je.STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(je.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_io_read_fraction DESC

question: For each disk device across all completed FileRead and FileWrite events combined, what is the total read operation count, total write operation count, total bytes read, total bytes written, average read efficiency ratio (effective read throughput divided by declared disk_read_bw), average write efficiency ratio (effective write throughput divided by declared disk_write_bw), and the read-to-write byte ratio — revealing which disk devices face the heaviest mixed I/O workloads and whether any disks are systematically biased toward reads or writes?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS read_count, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS write_count, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL) > 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / (CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) ELSE NULL END) AS avg_read_efficiency_ratio, AVG(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL) > 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / (CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) ELSE NULL END) AS avg_write_efficiency_ratio, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END), 0) AS read_to_write_byte_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE STATE = 'Finished' AND EVENT IN ('FileRead', 'FileWrite') GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') ORDER BY total_bytes_read + total_bytes_written DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by link_load tier at completion (under 0.25, 0.25 to 0.50, 0.50 to 0.75, and over 0.75), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average declared bandwidth, average throughput efficiency ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth), and number of distinct source-to-destination routes — revealing how network congestion level at the moment of transfer completion correlates with actual delivery performance relative to declared capacity?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.25 THEN 'under_0.25' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.50 THEN '0.25_to_0.50' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.75 THEN '0.50_to_0.75' ELSE 'over_0.75' END AS link_load_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_throughput_efficiency_ratio, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') || '->' || json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site')) AS distinct_route_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY link_load_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL))

question: For each site, comparing site_cpu_util and grid_cpu_util recorded at JobExecution Started events versus JobExecution Finished events for the same jobs, what is the matched job count, average site_cpu_util at start, average site_cpu_util at finish, average change in site_cpu_util (finish minus start), average grid_cpu_util at start, average grid_cpu_util at finish, and average change in grid_cpu_util — revealing whether CPU pressure at each site tends to build up or ease over the course of job execution and which sites experience the largest CPU load drift between job launch and completion?
sql: SELECT json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_start, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_finish, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_change, AVG(CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_start, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_finish, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_change FROM EVENTS s JOIN EVENTS f ON s.JOB_ID = f.JOB_ID AND f.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND f.STATE = 'Finished' WHERE s.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND s.STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY json_extract(s.METADATA, '$.site') ORDER BY avg_site_cpu_util_change DESC
